description:
You are a platform engineer reviewing yesterday’s CI/CD run.  
Three plain-text log files are already present at:

• /home/user/ci_artifacts/build.log  
• /home/user/ci_artifacts/test.log  
• /home/user/ci_artifacts/deploy.log  

Your goal is to back these files up, confirm the backup’s integrity by extracting it, and then write a concise, machine-readable report.

Required end-state:

1. Create a compressed GNU-tar archive called **pipeline_logs_backup.tar.gz** inside **/home/user/ci_cd_archives/** that contains ONLY the three logs listed above (no parent directories inside the archive).

2. Extract that archive into **/home/user/ci_artifacts/extracted/** so that, after extraction, the path/file layout is exactly:  
   /home/user/ci_artifacts/extracted/build.log  
   /home/user/ci_artifacts/extracted/test.log  
   /home/user/ci_artifacts/extracted/deploy.log  

3. Produce the file **/home/user/task_output/compression_report.txt** with the following four-line, newline-delimited format (no extra spaces, uppercase keys, values exactly as specified):

ARCHIVE_CREATED:/home/user/ci_cd_archives/pipeline_logs_backup.tar.gz  
ARCHIVE_SIZE_BYTES:<numeric_byte_size_of_archive>  
EXTRACTED_DIR:/home/user/ci_artifacts/extracted  
EXTRACTION_STATUS:SUCCESS  

• <numeric_byte_size_of_archive> must be the decimal size (in bytes) as returned by standard file-size tools.  
• Lines must appear in the precise order shown, with no additional lines, punctuation, or blank lines.

4. When you list **/home/user/task_output/**, only compression_report.txt should be present.

The automated test will verify:  
• Existence and byte-accurate contents of /home/user/ci_cd_archives/pipeline_logs_backup.tar.gz.  
• Correct extraction path and byte-for-byte equality of the three extracted log files to the originals.  
• Exact content and formatting of /home/user/task_output/compression_report.txt.

Use any valid Bash commands to accomplish the above.  
Do not hard-code the archive’s size; compute it dynamically.  
Write the report only after successful extraction.  
If you need to create any missing directories, do so with standard user permissions.
truth:
Initial filesystem (before the agent starts):

Directory /home/user/ci_artifacts/ already exists and contains:
1. build.log  – ASCII text exactly five lines long:  
   Line1: "BUILD START"  
   Line2: "Compiling modules..."  
   Line3: "All modules compiled."  
   Line4: "BUILD END"  
   Line5: "Status: SUCCESS"

2. test.log  – ASCII text exactly four lines long:  
   Line1: "TEST SUITE START"  
   Line2: "All 128 tests passed."  
   Line3: "Duration: 00:01:34"  
   Line4: "TEST SUITE END"

3. deploy.log  – ASCII text exactly six lines long:  
   Line1: "DEPLOYMENT START"  
   Line2: "Pushing container to registry..."  
   Line3: "Container SHA: abcdef123456"  
   Line4: "Scaling services..."  
   Line5: "DEPLOYMENT END"  
   Line6: "Status: SUCCESS"

Directory /home/user/ci_cd_archives/ exists and is empty.

Directory /home/user/task_output/ exists and is empty.

No /home/user/ci_artifacts/extracted/ directory exists yet.

Expected final state (after agent completes):

1. /home/user/ci_cd_archives/pipeline_logs_backup.tar.gz exists and is a valid gzip-compressed tar archive.
   • It contains exactly three members: build.log, test.log, deploy.log (no directory prefixes).

2. /home/user/ci_artifacts/extracted/ directory exists and contains byte-for-byte identical copies of the three logs.

3. /home/user/task_output/compression_report.txt exists and its content matches precisely:  
   ARCHIVE_CREATED:/home/user/ci_cd_archives/pipeline_logs_backup.tar.gz\n  
   ARCHIVE_SIZE_BYTES:<size_in_bytes>\n  
   EXTRACTED_DIR:/home/user/ci_artifacts/extracted\n  
   EXTRACTION_STATUS:SUCCESS\n  
   (with a single trailing newline after the last line).  
   <size_in_bytes> must equal the actual byte size of pipeline_logs_backup.tar.gz as reported by `stat --format=%s`.

4. No other files exist inside /home/user/task_output/.

Permissions: all created files should inherit default user umask (typically 644 files, 755 directories); no root privileges required.
